primary_diagnosis.name: Neuropathic POTS with severe dependent venous pooling (acrocyanosis)

pathophysiology_summary: Neuropathic POTS involves failure of lower-limb vasoconstriction on standing, causing dependent venous pooling, acrocyanosis and cerebral hypoperfusion with orthostatic tachycardia and near-syncope that resolve instantly on recumbency/elevation. This mimics bilateral DVT, acute limb ischaemia, cardiogenic syncope and hypovolaemia. Reassuring features are bilateral, symmetric, elevation-resolving cyanosis with palpable equal pulses and soft non-tender calves, no single fixed ischaemic limb, and no true syncope/chest pain. However, telehealth cannot examine the legs/pulses or obtain an ECG, and the fall risk is high, so urgent in-person review (legs + ECG) plus fall-safety and supportive compression/salt-fluids is required — with 000 for a single pale/cold/pulseless leg, true syncope or chest pain.

differential.evidence_basis: Dependent cyanosis resolving on elevation on a known POTS background.
Purple painful legs demand DVT screening.
Screen for a single pale/cold/pulseless leg.
Bilateral elevation-resolving cyanosis, no ischaemic/DVT leg, no true syncope.
Low probability but not excludable without a leg exam.
Distribution problem rather than absolute volume loss (moist mucosa/normal turgor).
Working telehealth diagnosis: neuropathic POTS pooling needing urgent in-person leg/ECG review, fall-safety and supportive compression/salt-fluids.

red_flags.description: A single leg becoming fixedly pale, cold, numb or pulseless (acute limb ischaemia)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Acute arterial occlusion threatens the limb and needs emergency care.

red_flags.description: Fixed unilateral calf swelling/tenderness with DVT risk (DVT)

red_flags.clinical_significance: Would raise a cardiac cause needing urgent assessment.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Not screening for a single ischaemic leg or unilateral DVT features.
Sending bilateral elevation-resolving cyanosis to ED by ambulance as 'clots' when it fits pooling and needs in-person review.
Starting tadalafil/midodrine over chat before assessment.
Not addressing near-syncope/fall risk and poor intake while isolated.

risk_scores.expected_score_or_tier: Orthostatic tachycardia with dependent pooling; no ischaemic/DVT limb — POTS pattern
risk_scores.clinical_implication: Supports in-person leg/ECG review with supportive management and fall-safety.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Leg examination, pulse palpation and Doppler/ultrasound
Reliable postural vitals and cardiac auscultation
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Screen limb-ischaemia/DVT and cardiac features; advise compression/salt-fluids/counter-manoeuvres and fall-safety; arrange urgent in-person leg + ECG review; escalate to 000 for a single pale/cold/pulseless leg, true syncope or chest pain.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Either missing an ischaemic limb/DVT or over-treating pooling with remote vasoactives/ambulance.

guidelines_referenced.reference: Heart Rhythm Society expert consensus on POTS
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Neuropathic POTS is managed with compression, volume expansion and reconditioning, with medications specialist-guided.
A pale, cold, pulseless limb is a surgical emergency; suspected DVT requires assessment and imaging.
